name: Start Up Business Loans Seattle
description_short: RNC Bridge & Private Hard Money Lender provides startup and real estate investment loans from $100K–$10M with 70% LTV and 2–3 year terms at 7.99–12% interest rates.
description_long:
Start Up Business Loans Seattle operates as RNC Bridge & Private Hard Money Lender, a private lending company specializing in bridge loans and hard money financing for real estate investors and business ventures. The company positions itself as an alternative to traditional bank lending, targeting borrowers who need fast capital or don't meet conventional bank underwriting standards. Based on the website content, they have financed hundreds of clients in startup and real estate scenarios.

The company offers hard money loans ranging from $100,000 to $10,000,000 with loan-to-value ratios up to 70%, interest rates between 7.99% and 12%, and terms of 2–3 years with renewal options. Their stated application process involves three steps: prequalification consultation, document submission and underwriting, and loan disbursement. They claim instant approval processes, no prepayment penalties, and the ability to close deals quickly. Target use cases include fix-and-flip projects, rental property purchases, property repositioning, land development, construction, and commercial real estate investment.

The company differentiates itself through positioning hard money lending as faster and more flexible than traditional mortgages, particularly for borrowers with credit scores below bank guidelines or those requiring non-standard loan structures. They advertise low approval criteria, the ability to consider multiple property types, and specialized expertise in bridge lending. However, they explicitly acknowledge in their FAQ that hard money loans carry higher interest rates than traditional mortgages due to greater risk inherent in short-term real estate projects.

Potential borrowers should understand that while fast funding and flexible approval are genuine advantages for investors, the cost structure is significantly higher than traditional financing, and the company notes additional costs and fees may be discovered during the process. This product is designed for experienced real estate investors with substantial equity and ability to weather project delays, not for traditional home buyers or cash-strapped consumers seeking affordable financing.
address: Norton Building, 801 2nd Ave Suite 700, Seattle, WA 98104

company_info.headquarters: Norton Building, 801 2nd Ave Suite 700, Seattle, WA 98104

pros: Loan amounts up to $10,000,000 with $100K minimum, suitable for serious real estate projects and developments
Fast approval and funding process compared to traditional bank mortgages for time-sensitive opportunities
70% LTV maximum ratio is competitive for hard money lending industry standards
No prepayment penalties, allowing borrowers to refinance or pay off early without additional costs
Flexible approval criteria for borrowers with credit scores below traditional bank guidelines
2–3 year terms with renewal options provide medium-term capital solutions
Specialized in multiple property types and real estate scenarios (fix-and-flip, construction, multifamily, commercial)
cons: Interest rates of 7.99–12% are substantially higher than traditional mortgage rates (typically 3–7%), significantly increasing borrowing costs
Requires 30–40% down payment for experienced borrowers and higher for first-time borrowers, limiting accessibility
Higher total cost of capital due to additional fees and costs that may not be fully transparent upfront, as acknowledged in their FAQ
Hard money loans are explicitly described as riskier and more suitable for experienced investors, not appropriate for traditional homebuyers
Limited transparency on exact fee structure and total cost of borrowing in website disclosures
best_for: Real estate investors conducting fix-and-flip or value-add property projects with tight timelines
Commercial property investors needing quick capital for acquisition or development
Borrowers with credit impairment or non-traditional income who cannot qualify for traditional bank mortgages
Experienced investors seeking bridge financing between property sales or rapid deployment capital
diagnosis: Start Up Business Loans Seattle is best for experienced real estate investors and commercial borrowers who prioritize speed and flexibility over cost, and who have substantial equity to invest as collateral. The primary caveat is that hard money financing is significantly more expensive than traditional lending—borrowers should expect 7.99–12% rates plus additional fees and should only pursue this option when the time-sensitive nature of the deal or credit constraints make traditional financing unavailable.
